name: Philly Joe Jones
abstract: Joseph Rudolph "Philly Joe" Jones (July 15, 1923 – August 30, 1985) was an American jazz drummer. He was noted for being able to adjust his style of playing to allow for any group or individual's needs.

concerts.label: Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker
Kool Jazz Festival: Bill Evans-A Tribute
Kool Jazz Festival: Charlie Haden's Liberation Music Orchestra/Philly Joe Jones' Dameronia

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: I did a gig at the Blue Coronet in Brooklyn with Philly Joe and Paul Chambers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: Do you remember that? He got a job as a motorman... Max Roach used to come over and ride a route with him... When we did that album... Philly was the drummer... We worked with Bass Ashford... I was in Atlantic City with Clifford Brown, Tadd Dameron, Philly Joe, and all those
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Miles Davis band--we had and Trane, Red, Paul and Philly Joe... Red Garland, Paul Chambers and Philly Joe Jones, with John Coltrane, were members of the Miles Davis quintet, 1955-1956.

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: I played it every Sunday from 5 to 9, Lou Donaldson and the Red Garland Trio... Philly Joe didn’t like Betty Carter... I went and I said, “Joe, listen. You can’t do that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Barrett
about_quotes.evidence: the great drummers, Jo Jones and Sid Catlett and Philly Joe Jones and John Coltrane, I don’t know, but they all... they had complete control of the beat.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Barrett interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: Tadd hired me, Clifford Brown, Johnny Coles, Cecil Payne, Jymie Merritt, Philly Joe Jones, and a trombone player. We were playing music for the show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
